title: Runner removal: delete the built-in tk run execution stack
description: Execution moved to Claude Code orchestration (docs/claude-code-swarm-architecture.md; the ticks skill explicitly does not use tk run). The built-in runner stack (~17-19k LOC, ~15% of the codebase) is unused: cmd/tk/cmd/run.go plus internal/engine, internal/wrapup, internal/output, internal/runstate, internal/runlog, internal/budget, and internal/wave/runner.go. Separately, internal/recovery (zero importers) and internal/streamtest (6-line hello-world) are already dead, along with stale top-level dirs (.ticker/, .tickflow-bin/, prototypes/, benchmarks/). One coupling needs a human decision: the board imports internal/agent and internal/runrecord to watch .tick/logs/records/ for live run output (internal/tickboard/server/server.go:19-21, watcher.go:11) - a pipeline only tk run feeds, so it is dead under orchestration; it can be deleted or repurposed as an orchestrator telemetry channel. KEEP regardless: internal/wave/wave.go (tk graph + internal/query/roadmap.go), internal/worktree (tk merge), internal/context (tk context), internal/gc, internal/config.
notes:
2026-06-10 21:33 - RETRO REPORT (epic close):
Learnings promoted — .tick/learnings.md (71/150 lines): main.go dual-registration entry updated (now guarded by TestLegacyDispatchCoversAllCobraCommands); new entry on in-process cobra test quirks (use ExecuteArgs/ExecuteArgsContext only). No skill/process promotions this epic — the BLOCKED-stop and review-fix-loop doctrines worked as written.
Verification (outside-in, built binary) — tk run/resume/checkpoints: unknown command. tk context: dispatch fixed and routing (pre-existing bug found by review). tk board: boots, serves /api/ticks + embedded UI 200, fail-fast on occupied explicit port (blocker fix verified empirically on macOS). Cloud sync restored behind tk board --cloud via self-contained client. Full Go suite + UI suite green; gofmt now clean repo-wide.
Scope evolution — grew by 4 ticks during the run: 8v9 + zbj (pi harness deletion per human decision), 49i (board entrypoint orphaned by runner deletion - caught by vst agent), plus rescope of 0u6 when its agent correctly stopped on live importers (tk context, tk migrate, verification badges) - consistent with wwv verdict wording 'once importer-free'.
Net diff — approx -39.5k/+1.2k lines: runner tower, recovery, streamtest, pi harness, TickflowLease, board run pipeline, stranded APIs, schema cluster, 5 stale dirs, operator docs.
Accepted/deferred — dcg (awaiting input): retire internal/agent+internal/runrecord vs accept as shared (tk context/migrate/badges depend); xdq: worker test env crash + stale /agent tests + worker tests missing from CI; board wildcard-bind localhost hardening (review suggestion, pre-existing behavior); UI tsc --noEmit pre-existing failures (offered to user, undecided).
Integration status — awaiting stacked PR onto epic/idc (PR #11 still open).
2026-06-10 21:35 - INCIDENT (retro addendum): ips's original close was lost - the vst implementer ran git-restore against the shared checkout (admitted in its report) which wiped uncommitted tick state; orchestrator's 'tree clean' verification read the wipe as healthy. Lesson promoted to skill + learnings: commit tick state IMMEDIATELY after mutating it, before any merge or launch; treat a clean tree after an agent confession as suspect, not safe.
closed_reason: Completed: built-in runner fully removed (~39.5k LOC deleted) and replaced surface restored (tk board with --cloud); all 9 ticks closed via Claude orchestration; retro in epic notes; awaiting stacked PR onto epic/idc
